IN ADDITION TO THE REASONS PROVIDED IN THE RULING ON CO-DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER, THERE IS NO ACTUAL CONTROVERSY BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND IMPAC. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT IMPAC HAS A CURRENT CLAIM TO THE PROPERTY AND PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT IMPAC CLAIMS TO BE A MEMBER OF MERS. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, INC., OCWEN LOAN SERVICING, LLC, DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY'S TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION AS ALLEGED. PLAINTIFF ATTEMPTS TO CHALLENGE DEUTSCHE BANK'S AUTHORITY/STANDING TO FORECLOSE PRE-FORECLOSURE. THIS CHALLENGE IS NOT ALLOWED UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW. PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT THAT HER ACTION IS PROPER BECAUSE IT IS A "PRE-SALE" LAWSUIT "BASED ON ALLEGED MISCONDUCT BY THE DEFENDANTS SUPPORTED BY SPECIFIC FACTS" FAILS AS A MATTER OF LAW. PLAINTIFF ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT SHOW THAT HER LOAN ENDED UP WHERE IT WAS SUPPOSED TO, WITH THE TRUSTEE OF IMPAC SECURED ASSETS TRUST 2006-6 (SEE PARAGRAPH 21, 22). THE ORIGINAL DEED OF TRUST ALLOWED FOR THE LOAN TO BE TRANSFERRED AND DESIGNATED MERS AS A NOMINEE FOR THE LENDER AND LENDER'S SUCCESSORS AND ASSIGNS. HERRERA V. FED. NAT'L MORTGAGE ASS'N (2012) 205 CAL.APP.4TH 1495, DISAPPROVED ON OTHER GROUNDS. AS THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY ALLOWED, THE PLAINTIFF COULD HAVE STATED A DECLARATORY RELIEF ACTION TO DETERMINE WHO PLAINTIFF IS SUPPOSED TO REPAY THE LOAN TO: THE ORIGINAL LENDER, ONE OF THE INTERIM TRANSFEREES, OR SECURED ASSETS TRUST 2006-6. PLAINTIFF CHOSE TO NOT MAKE SUCH AMENDMENT AND INSTEAD IS SEEKING RELIEF PROHIBITED BY CALIFORNIA LAW, WHICH INCLUDES NON-JUDICIAL FORECLOSURE SCHEME. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS IMPAC FUNDING CORPORATION, IMPAC SECURED ASSET CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. In addition to the reasons provided in the ruling on co-defendants' demurrer, there is no actual controversy between Plaintiff and IMPAC. Plaintiff does not allege that IMPAC has a current claim to the property and Plaintiff does not allege that IMPAC claims to be a member of MERS. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, INC., OCWEN LOAN SERVICING, LLC, DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY'S TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action as alleged. Plaintiff attempts to challenge Deutsche Bank's authority/standing to foreclose pre-foreclosure. This challenge is not allowed under California law. Plaintiff's argument that her action is proper because it is a "pre-sale" lawsuit "based on alleged misconduct by the defendants supported by specific facts" fails as a matter of law. Plaintiff allegations in the complaint show that her loan ended up where it was supposed to, with the trustee of IMPAC Secured Assets Trust 2006-6 (See paragraph 21, 22). The original Deed of Trust allowed for the loan to be transferred and designated MERS as a nominee for the Lender and Lender's successors and assigns. Herrera v. Fed. Nat'l Mortgage Ass'n (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 1495, disapproved on other grounds. As the Court has previously allowed, the Plaintiff could have stated a declaratory relief action to determine who Plaintiff is supposed to repay the loan to: the original lender, one of the interim transferees, or Secured Assets Trust 2006-6. Plaintiff chose to not make such amendment and instead is seeking relief prohibited by California law, which includes non-judicial foreclosure scheme. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
